name: Faro des Moscarter and Portinatx — circular route through the north of Ibiza
name_original: Faro des Moscarter y Portinatx — circular por el norte de Ibiza
region_intro: Hiking around Sant Joan De Labritja offers diverse and unspoiled natural landscapes in the northern part of Ibiza. The region is characterized by rugged terrain, rolling hills, and dense forests, including pine, olive, and almond trees. Hikers can find coastal paths with dramatic cliffs and secluded coves, as well as inland trails traversing the unique Torrent de Labritja. These natural features provide varied hiking terrain with options for different elevation and difficulty levels. Best hiking trails around Sant Joan De Labritja The most popular hiking route is Faro des Moscarter and Portinatx — circular route through the north of Ibiza, a 4.6 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 1 minute to complete. This moderate route offers coastal views and leads towards Portinatx. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cave Entrance – Rocky Forest Trail loop from Cala de Sant Vicent, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.9 km) path. This trail winds through rocky forest terrain with significant elevation gain. Local hikers also love the Llevant Cave – Natural Bridge loop from Cala de Xuclar, a 2.2 miles (3.6 km) trail leading through coastal areas and natural rock formations, often completed in about 1 hour 3 minutes. Hiking around Sant Joan De Labritja is defined by rugged coastal paths, dense pine forests, and rolling hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging ability levels. The routes in Sant Joan De Labritja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1600 reviews. More than 7000 hikers have used komoot to explore Sant Joan De Labritja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: Sant Joan De Labritja offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 100 routes available. These range from easy coastal strolls to more challenging inland treks, catering to different fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: The region is characterized by diverse and unspoiled natural landscapes. You can expect rugged terrain, rolling hills, and dense forests, including fragrant pine, olive, and almond trees. Many trails offer breathtaking views of the coastline, dramatic cliffs, and secluded coves. Inland, you might encounter the unique Torrent de Labritja, which, though often dry, reveals a bed of green vegetation.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: Yes, there are numerous easy trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1021043" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llevant Cave – Natural Bridge loop from Cala de Xuclar&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.6 km trail that takes about an hour to complete, leading through coastal areas and natural rock formations. Overall, about half of the trails in the area are classified as easy.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for while hiking?
A: Hikers can discover a variety of natural features and landmarks. The area is known for its dramatic cliffs and secluded coves. You might encounter the unique Torrent de Labritja, or explore natural rock formations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/473420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moscarter Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent landmark often included in coastal routes, offering spectacular views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: Yes, many routes in Sant Joan De Labritja are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000134993/faro-des-moscarter-and-portinatx-circular-route-through-the-north-of-ibiza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro des Moscarter and Portinatx — circular route through the north of Ibiza&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.5 km trail offering coastal views.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: The trails in Sant Joan De Labritja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1600 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the unspoiled natural beauty, the diverse landscapes ranging from dense forests to dramatic coastlines, and the tranquility found away from more bustling areas. The well-maintained paths and stunning viewpoints are also often highlighted.

Q: Are there any caves or interesting geological sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region features several caves and unique geological formations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7188146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llevant Cave&lt;/a&gt; is mentioned in one of the easy routes, and the area also has other notable caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5847493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova des Forn&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1757634" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Blanca Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;. The famous Can Marçà caves, with their green pools and waterfall, are also nearby.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sant Joan De Labritja?
A: Sant Joan De Labritja is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer treks through forests and along the coast. Even in winter, the mild climate can be suitable for hiking. During the hotter summer months, it's best to hike early in the morning or late afternoon to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of secluded coves or beaches?
A: Absolutely. Many coastal paths provide stunning views of secluded coves and crystal-clear waters. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000134993/faro-des-moscarter-and-portinatx-circular-route-through-the-north-of-ibiza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro des Moscarter and Portinatx — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers coastal vistas towards Portinatx. Cala Xarraca is also renowned for its clear turquoise waters and rocky formations, accessible via trails like Ruta de Cala Xarraca (PR-EI-103).

Q: Can I find challenging hikes with significant elevation gain in the area?
A: While many trails are moderate or easy, there are options for more challenging hikes. The region's rugged terrain and rolling hills provide opportunities for significant elevation changes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1464687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave Entrance – Rocky Forest Trail loop from Cala de Sant Vicent&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.9 km path that winds through rocky forest terrain with notable elevation gain.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through traditional Ibicenco landscapes?
A: Yes, some trails offer a glimpse into the traditional Ibicenco landscape. The Ruta des Camí Vell de Portinatx (PR-EI-102), for instance, follows an old path showcasing lush pine forests and traditional Ibicenco farms. The Es Amunts circular trail also connects Sant Joan de Labritja with Cala de Sant Vicent, passing by old wells and traditional houses.

Q: Are there any specific trails known for their scenic viewpoints?
A: Many trails in Sant Joan De Labritja offer spectacular viewpoints due to the rugged coastline and hilly terrain. The coastal path from Cala d'en Serra to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/473420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moscarter Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; is particularly noted for its cliff-top views. Additionally, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3809407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ses Torretes Peak&lt;/a&gt; is a designated viewpoint in the area.
tips: Super beautiful bay with a great beach. A bit out of the way, so often not so crowded.

The northernmost point of Ibiza, made clear by this beautiful lighthouse.
The coast here, however, is alive. You can see that the sea 🌊 “gnaws” on the coast, similar to the Rügen chalk cliffs. The coastal edge is breaking off. This is also the case with the area of the lighthouse. Sometime…
Very impressive and particularly impressive cliff. Otherwise there is also still at San Vicente.
The coastal town of Portinatx is located in the extreme northeast of Ibiza.
